steps.title: Distribute the coefficient to each term
steps.content: The expression $9(x^{2}+6)$ has the coefficient 9 multiplying a binomial. Using the distributive property, we distribute 9 to both terms inside the parentheses: $$9(x^{2}+6) = 9 \cdot x^{2} + 9 \cdot 6$$

steps.title: Simplify the products
steps.content: Compute each product: $9 \cdot x^{2} = 9x^{2}$ and $9 \cdot 6 = 54$. Combining these gives us $$9x^{2} + 54$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
